stories.title: 2026 CNBC Disruptor 50 list: Why Anthropic was No. 1 in this year's rankings
stories.summary: Anthropic, posting explosive growth for its powerful AI systems that enterprises trust, has leapfrogged OpenAI on the CNBC Disruptor 50 list as the new No. 1.

stories.title: OpenAI announces new Guaranteed Capacity offering for customers to secure compute
stories.summary: Altman said OpenAI will make sure it leaves enough capacity available for its products like ChatGPT and its coding assistant Codex.

stories.title: Fed to hike? When traders see a rate increase coming
stories.summary: Traders on prediction market platforms see increasing odds that an interest rate hike from the central bank comes by July 2027.

stories.title: Analog Devices to Acquire Empower in $1.5 Billion Transaction
stories.summary: Analog Devices Inc. agreed to acquire closely held Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 billion in cash as the need skyrockets for efficient chips to power data centers required for artificial intelligence.

stories.title: Ross Gerber Says AI Efficiency Gains Already 'Amazing'
stories.summary: Gerber Kawasaki Wealth and Investment Management co-founder and CEO Ross Gerber previews Nvidia's first-quarter earnings report and what it might signal for the AI industry on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Google debuts new AI models, personal AI agents in effort to keep pace with OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.summary: Google made a number of AI announcements at its annual developer conference, rolling out more-advanced models and agentic tools for its expansive user base.

stories.title: Google to release smart glasses and add AI ‘agents’ to search engine
stories.summary: CEO Sundar Pichai says features powered by new Gemini model will close gap with Anthropic and OpenAI

stories.title: Google Executive Says ‘Moonshot’ Clean Energy Goal Is Intact
stories.summary: A senior executive at Alphabet Inc.’s Google said the company isn’t abandoning plans to run its business on 24/7 carbon-free energy by 2030, even as the buildout of data centers for AI raises questions about the feasibility of such a goal.

stories.title: Big Treasury Block Sales Drive ‘Capitulation’ Selloff in Bonds
stories.summary: A series of large sales in US Treasury futures exacerbated a selloff in the $31 trillion government debt market, as concern about resurgent inflation pushed investors to price in higher interest rates.

stories.title: Trump endorses ‘Maga warrior’ Ken Paxton in Texas Senate run-off
stories.summary: President’s tapping of controversial candidate could give Democrats an opening in November’s midterm elections

stories.title: Anthropic and U.S. government to face off in DC court over blacklisting of AI company
stories.summary: Anthropic sued the Defense Department in March after the agency declared the artificial intelligence startup a supply chain risk.

stories.title: Affordability in Focus As Georgia Heads To The Polls
stories.summary: Metro Atlanta Chamber CEO Katie Kirkpatrick said Georgia has invested heavily in the power grid and water supply that will help the state cope with the expansion of data centers needed to drive artificial intelligence development. She also discussed the Georgia economy as voting continues. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Wall Street prepares for boom in tech IPOs after Cerebras’ success
stories.summary: Chip designer’s $6.4bn raise signals demand ahead of huge listings expected from SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic

stories.title: Wall Street Watchdogs Pause Some Cyber Exams After Mythos Shock
stories.summary: US regulators are pausing some cyber-related examinations of the largest banks, giving the lenders more breathing room as the firms dig into the risks exposed by Anthropic PBC’s new Mythos AI model.

stories.title: Crypto Crime Escalates With Kidnappings, Cons and Human Coercion
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Isabelle Lee joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." After a year of kidnappings, assaults and armed home invasions targeting cryptocurrency holders, the industry is racing to harden its defenses. Conferences are beefing up security. Private firms serving crypto holders say demand has surged. Exchanges are protecting their executives. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: AI Data Center Borrowing Rapidly Climbs Wall Street’s Risk List
stories.summary: Borrowing tied to the AI data-center boom is quickly climbing Wall Street’s list of potential credit threats, as investors increasingly worry that the breakneck pace of financing could sow the seeds of the next market shock.

stories.title: Dollar 'Extremely More Powerful' with US Bill: Thorn
stories.summary: Alex Thorn, head of firmwide research at Galaxy Digital, joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." Last week the Senate Banking Committee advanced landmark crypto legislation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Surging Bond Yields Add to Pressures Building for Fed’s Warsh
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh is poised to take the helm of the US central bank later this week facing pressure from the White House to deliver interest-rate cuts and growing resolve from his colleagues to stand pat. Now the bond market is piling on.

stories.title: Appeals Court Skeptical Anthropic Can Block US Supply-Risk Label
stories.summary: A federal appeals court appeared skeptical of Anthropic’s bid to block the Pentagon from declaring that the company poses a supply-chain risk to US national security, a move that led to a ban on government use of its artificial intelligence technology.

stories.title: Musk Turns to Loyalists to Rebuild xAI Ahead of SpaceX IPO
stories.summary: Who's leading Musk's AI company? He's turned to leaders at his other companies to rebuild the startup.

stories.title: Google, Blackstone to Create AI Cloud Firm
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Google's decision to create an AI cloud business with Blackstone which will run on Google's homegrown AI chips. Plus, the legal battle between Elon Musk and his OpenAI co-founders ends with a jury ruling that Musk waited too long to sue. And, Meta bets big on Louisiana for the world's biggest AI facility, as it plans AI-related layoffs this week. (So

stories.title: Boaz Weinstein’s Hedge Fund Takes Aim at Impax’s ‘Terrible’ Run
stories.summary: As Boaz Weinstein’s hedge fund, Saba Capital Management, takes on one of the most high-profile environmental investment trusts in Britain, the standoff is morphing into a particularly brutal moment for ESG.

stories.title: Trump claims Xi will not arm Iran as strike threat looms
stories.summary: US president warns Tehran has ‘two or three days’ to reach a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

stories.title: Anthropic hires OpenAI co-founder Andrej Karpathy, former Tesla AI leader
stories.summary: Andrej Karpathy, an artificial intelligence researcher who co-founded OpenAI before getting poached by Tesla, said he's joining Anthropic.

stories.title: US Senate Signals Intensifying Opposition to Trump’s War in Iran
stories.summary: The Republican-led US Senate signaled mounting opposition to continuing the Iran war in a procedural vote Tuesday, reflecting deepening political unease over a foreign conflict that is taking a mounting financial toll on Americans.

stories.title: 30-year Treasury yield tops 5.19%, highest since before the financial crisis
stories.summary: Global bond markets remained on edge as traders monitored central bank responses to renewed inflation fears.

stories.title: Itochu Takes Over AirLife’s Japan Sales in Healthcare Expansion
stories.summary: A subsidiary of Japanese trading house Itochu Corp. will take over SunMed Group Holdings’ Japan sales operations, as the US medical equipment maker struggles to remain profitable in the country due to the weak yen and inflationary pressure.

stories.title: Massive Options Bet Rattles Oil Market On Edge Over Iran War
stories.summary: A huge options bet Tuesday on Brent crude prices plunging rattled oil traders already on high alert for unusual flows, as Iran war headlines continue to whipsaw prices and regulators probe suspicious trading.

stories.title: The Worst-Case Climate Scenario Is Dead — and Still Spurring Debate
stories.summary: RCP8.5 is officially retired, scientists say.

stories.title: Mortgage rates surge to highest level since July
stories.summary: Mortgage rates continued to move higher Tuesday on uncertainty over the war with Iran. They are at the highest level since last July.

stories.title: Pentagon taps Shield AI for low-cost drone program as Iran war accelerates demand for cheap drones
stories.summary: Shield AI is fresh off a $1 billion funding round, which valued the company at $12.7 billion

stories.title: Scientists Ditch Worst-Case Climate Scenario, With Ridicule From Trump
stories.summary: Global warming skeptics, including the US president, have celebrated the end of the scenario known as RCP8.5. Experts say it was retired for being outdated, not wrong.

stories.title: Oil ticks lower as investors weigh mixed signals from Trump on whether U.S. will resume Iran war
stories.summary: Oil prices fell after U.S. President Donald Trump said he would postpone a planned military strike on Iran following requests from key Middle Eastern leaders.

stories.title: Chainalysis CEO Tracks Prediction Market Insider Trading
stories.summary: Jonathan Levin, founder and CEO of Chainalysis, joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." Polymarket is partnering with blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis to help police its platform as prediction markets grapple with increased scrutiny over insider trading. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Stablecoins Allow Global Expansion: Native Markets CEO
stories.summary: Mary-Catherine Lader, co-founder and CEO of Native Markets, joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." They discuss Hyperliquid, stablecoin growth, and US legislation. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Parts of Clarity Act 'Too Vague': ABA's Ybarra
stories.summary: Brooke Ybarra, SVP of innovation and strategy at American Bankers Association (ABA), joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." Last week the Senate Banking Committee advanced landmark crypto legislation despite a late push from banking groups. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Kraken's Parent See Earnings Tumble; Cuts Jobs
stories.summary: Bloomberg's Olga Kharif joins Scarlet Fu and Tim Stenovec on "Bloomberg Crypto." Kraken has cut some staff to reduce costs and reportedly may not go public as soon as initially expected. Payward, which operates crypto exchange Kraken, reported a steep decline in first-quarter adjusted earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Clarity Act Wins Senate Committee Vote, Crypto Wrench Attacks Surge | Bloomberg Crypto 5/19/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg Crypto" covers the people, transactions, and technology shaping the world of decentralized finance. Today's guests: Galaxy Digital Head of Firmwide Research Alex Thorn, American Bankers Association SVP of Innovation and Strategy Brooke Ybarra, Native Markets Co-Founder & CEO Mary-Catherine Lader, and Chainalsis Co-Founder & CEO Jonathan Levin. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Stocks Fall for Third Day as Semiconductor Shares Erase Rebound

stories.title: AMD CEO’s meeting with China’s vice-premier raises optimism about US AI chip imports
stories.summary: A recent meeting between Chinese Vice-Premier He Lifeng and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) CEO Lisa Su signals a potential loosening of US chip export policies, which could reopen high-end semiconductor sales, including artificial intelligence chips, to select Chinese firms, according to analysts. During their meeting on Monday, He reiterated that Beijing welcomed continued foreign investment, accor

stories.title: DeepSeek recruits former Jane Street engineer to catch up on AI agents, revenue race
stories.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence heavyweight DeepSeek has recruited a former Jane Street star engineer to its new AI “harness” team, as it looks to ensure it does not fall behind in the agentic AI era. Cui Tianyi joined the Hangzhou start-up in March, according to a post on his LinkedIn profile on Tuesday, after four years at Hong Kong-based quantitative trading firm TSY Capital, which he co-founde

stories.title: China Aims to Raise 6 Billion Yuan in HK Green Bond Sale
stories.summary: China is seeking to raise as much as 6 billion yuan ($882 million) in a green sovereign bond sale in Hong Kong next week, as the nation continues to build its presence in offshore debt markets and aims to fund climate action.

stories.title: NATO’s newest member Sweden announces $4 billion defense investment; Saab pops 5%
stories.summary: Sweden joined the military alliance in 2024 after it said that Russia's invasion of Ukraine had changed the security landscape.

stories.title: SpaceX Is Said to Plan to Buy Startup Cursor 30 Days After IPO
stories.summary: SpaceX expects to proceed with its acquisition of artificial intelligence coding startup Cursor 30 days after Elon Musk’s company begins trading publicly, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: SoftBank Insiders Worry About Masayoshi Son’s Bet on OpenAI
stories.summary: On today’s Big Take Asia podcast, why Masayoshi Son’s $60 billion investment in OpenAI is raising concerns inside SoftBank.

stories.title: SoftBank Founder’s Starstruck Bet on OpenAI Raises Concern
stories.summary: With more than $60 billion committed, some SoftBank insiders are growing uneasy over Son’s devotion to Altman.

stories.title: Google to release first smart glasses since Google Glass flop
stories.summary: The glasses will go on sale sometime in autumn and allow Google's artificial intelligence product to interact with a user.

stories.title: SoftBank’s $60 Billion OpenAI Bet Sparks Concerns
stories.summary: SoftBank’s Masayoshi Son has bet $60 billion on Sam Altman and OpenAI. Find out what’s driving his conviction — and why some see a risky, one‑sided dynamic emerging — on the Big Take Asia Podcast. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: College Kids Don't Want Your AI
stories.summary: Students are pushing back against the adoption of AI on campuses, with some organizing protests, petitions, and performance art to express their concerns about the technology's effects on their education and job prospects. Bloomberg’s Victor Swezey has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Hasbro Cancels Dungeons & Dragons Game From ‘Star Wars’ Veteran
stories.summary: Independent studio Giant Skull had announced a deal with Hasbro’s Wizards of the Coast last year.

stories.title: Saks Global CEO on Next Steps in Bankruptcy Process, Path To Growth
stories.summary: Saks Global CEO Geoffroy van Raemdonck discusses the progress the company has made since filing for bankruptcy. He discusses what the luxury retail space looks like for the brands that do business with Saks and what role brick and mortar stores will play going forward. He speaks with Romaine Bostick on “The Close.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Aspen Group Eyes New Investors to Address Debt as Earnings Slump
stories.summary: The Aspen Group, the owner of one of the largest dental care chains in the US, is exploring tapping outside investors to tackle the maturities of roughly $3 billion of loans due next year amid weaker earnings, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.title: What's the mortgage rate you'll be paying later this year? Probably higher
stories.summary: With mortgage rates reaching a record high on Tuesday, traders also increase likelihood that rates will go above 6.8%.

stories.title: Consumers Look For Bling for Buck In Lab Grown Gemstones

stories.title: New York’s Lawler Casts Doubt on More SALT Tax Relief This Year
stories.summary: Representative Mike Lawler, a key negotiator behind the temporary increase to the cap on state and local tax deductions, pushed back on efforts to further expand the tax break through legislation later this year.

stories.title: US Regulators Propose Reshaping Secret Bank Ratings Process
stories.summary: US regulators unveiled a proposal to overhaul how examiners secretly rate banks, a move likely to be cheered by lenders who have long griped about the process.

stories.title: JP Morgan’s Global CIO Lori Beer on the Pace of Change in the Tech Space
stories.summary: JP Morgan Global CIO Lori Beer discusses the acceleration of change in the tech space, even in the last year. She discusses the anxiety that can come from the ever-changing landscape, but recognizes that there is a huge demand for it as well. She speaks with Bloomberg Surveillance Host Lisa Abramowicz at the JP Morgan Tech, Media & Communications Conference. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Trump Refrains From Trading His Own Stocks, Vance Says
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance defended Donald Trump from criticism over his voluminous trading of financial assets, saying the president does not buy or sell his own stocks.

At the same time, the moribund office and retail property sectors were showing signs of improvement on the back of leasing activity despite

stories.title: Hong Kong stars Nick Cheung and William Chan join wave of upmarket home sales
stories.summary: Hong Kong actors Nick Cheung and William Chan have become the latest high-end homeowners to sell their units amid a strengthening recovery in the city’s luxury property market. Transactions in the segment more than doubled in the first quarter of the year, compared with the same period in 2025, according to consultancy CBRE. Cheung sold his flat at Grenville House in Mid-Levels for HK$132 million

stories.title: Standard Chartered lifts 2030 earnings target to 18% with Hong Kong at core: CEO Winters
stories.summary: Standard Chartered has raised its 2030 earnings target to 18 per cent by expanding wealth, cross-border banking and digital businesses, with Hong Kong at the centre of its growth story, according to global CEO Bill Winters.
